title: hieracium carolinianum f. glabrescens
intro: Hieracium carolinianum f. glabrescens, also known as the Carolina hawkweed or rock hawkweed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Hieracium carolinianum f. glabrescens is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be applied topically as a poultice or salve to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
